free_text: The elastic and inelastic scattering of 20-28 MeV
polarized protons by carbon.
With a polarized ion source, accelerator
produces a beam of 2.0E+8 proton per second, with
polarization of 30-50% at 30 or 50 MeV.
A 35 mg/cm**2 carbon foil mounted 25 cm behind the
degrades was used.
A 12 mg/cm**2 gold target was used as monitor.
The polarimeter target is a 65 mg/cm**2 carbon foil.
.Two NE102 plastic scintillator for monitor.
for main target
Two scintillation counter telescopes for
detection scattered protons and the polarimeter
target.
.This Uncertainty by Authors in Data Table.
The error given arise mainly from statistics, but
errors due to background subtraction, incomplete
separation of the peaks and uncertainties in the
polarimeter efficiency are occasionally appreciable.
The measurement of polarizations using a polarized
incident beam is relatively free from systematic errors
but the possibility of such errors arising from change
of beam position or direction on spin inversion- there
errors were negligible in the present experiment.
